2604.26762.title: Exploring the Potential of Probabilistic Transformer for Time Series Modeling: A Report on the ST-PT Framework
2604.26762.paper_summary: The Probabilistic Transformer (PT) establishes that the Transformer's self-attention plus its feed-forward block is mathematically equivalent to Mean-Field Variational Inference (MFVI) on a Conditional Random Field (CRF). Under this equivalence the Transformer ceases to be a black-box neural network and becomes a programmable factor graph: graph topology, factor potentials, and the message-passing schedule are all explicit and inspectable primitives that can be engineered. PT was originally developed for natural language and in this report we investigate its potential for time series. We first lift PT into the Spatial-Temporal Probabilistic Transformer (ST-PT) to repair PT's missing channel axis and weak per-step semantics, and adopt ST-PT as a shared cornerstone backbone. We then identify three distinct properties that PT/ST-PT offers as a factor-graph model and derive three Research Questions, one per property, that probe how each property can be exploited in time series: RQ1. The graph topology and potentials are direct programmable primitives. Can this be used to inject symbolic time-series priors into ST-PT through structural graph modifications, especially under data scarcity and noise? RQ2. The CRF's factor matrices are the operator's potentials. Can an external condition program these factor matrices on a per-sample basis, so that conditional generation becomes structural rather than feature-level modulation of a fixed one? RQ3. Each MFVI iteration is a Bayesian posterior update on the factor graph. Can this turn the latent transition of latent-space AutoRegressive (AR) forecasting from an opaque MLP into a principled posterior update, and can a CRF teacher distill its latents into the AR student to counter cumulative error? We give one empirical study per question. Together, these three studies position ST-PT as a programmable framework for time-series modeling.
2604.26762.paper_summary_zh: 概率Transformer（PT）確立了Transformer的自注意力加上其前饋區塊在數學上等同於條件隨機場（CRF）上的均場變分推斷（MFVI）。在這種等價性下，Transformer不再是一個黑箱神經網絡，而是成為一個可編程的因子圖：圖的拓撲、因子潛能和消息傳遞計劃都是明確且可檢查的原始元件，可以進行工程設計。PT 最初是為自然語言開發的，在本報告中，我們探討其在時間序列中的潛力。我們首先將 PT 提升為空間-時間概率Transformer（ST-PT），以修復 PT 缺失的通道軸和弱每步語義，並將 ST-PT 作為共享的基石骨幹。我們然後確定了 PT/ST-PT 作為因子圖模型所提供的三個不同特性，並推導出三個研究問題，每個特性一個，探討如何在時間序列中利用每個特性：RQ1。圖的拓撲和潛能是直接可編程的原始元件。這是否可以用來通過結構圖修改將符號時間序列先驗注入 ST-PT，特別是在數據稀缺和噪聲的情況下？RQ2。CRF 的因子矩陣是運算子的潛能。外部條件能否在每個樣本的基礎上編程這些因子矩陣，使得條件生成成為結構性而非固定一個的特徵級調制？RQ3。每次 MFVI 迭代都是因子圖上的貝葉斯後驗更新。這是否能將潛在空間自回歸（AR）預測的潛在轉換從不透明的 MLP 轉變為有原則的後驗更新，並且 CRF 教師能否將其潛在轉化為 AR 學生以抵消累積誤差？我們針對每個問題提供一項實證研究。這三項研究共同將 ST-PT 定位為一個可編程的時間序列建模框架。
2604.26762.author: Zhangzhi Xiong et.al.
2604.26762.authors: Zhangzhi Xiong, Haoyi Wu, You Wu, Shuqi Gu, Kan Ren, Kewei Tu
